# Functional Specification — Home Page as Direct Explainer (v2)

**Document:** Build-ready spec for `docs/index.html`, generated by `cartrack-ai-design-system/portal/docs/build_portal.py` → `body_home()` (~line 868) and `render_shell()` (~line 547) for shared CSS.
**Supersedes:** `home-page-product-narrative-functional-spec.md` (built and committed 2026-07-21, commit `dc0f430`). The user reviewed the live result and rejected the pitch/narrative structure (why-it-exists framing, proof-with-evidence block, condensed audience-path cards). This spec replaces that structure with a direct, mechanical explainer the user specified directly. See `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md` for the visual language (colours, type, radius, route-line motif) — unchanged and still in force.
**Constraint (explicit):** No changes to `design-system/` (tokens, components, `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md`, templates) or to `guides/index.html`'s role as the full-depth reference. This spec only changes what `body_home()` (and, for one small loader addition, the patterns-loading code around line 359) produces.
**Status:** Ready to build

---

## 1. Goal

Replace the current narrative-structured home page with a direct explainer: what this is, how to start using it in three steps, what's actually in the download, what the tokens/foundations model is, how many components exist, and how they compose into whole pages — in that order, ending with a repeated call to action. The user's own words: hero should introduce the AI-optimized design system with "start using it" (download) as the action; then explain, step by step, how someone actually gets from zero to a production-optimized UI prototype.

## 2. Non-goals

- No problem/why-it-exists framing block, no "proof, not promises" evidence block, no audience-persona path cards (Designers/Developers/AI agents) — all explicitly rejected by the user in favor of mechanics-first content. If a "who's it for" framing is wanted later, that's a new decision, not a revival of the prior spec.
- No change to `guides/index.html`, sidebar nav, filter box, or any component/pattern/foundation page.
- No change to the visual language established by `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md`.
- No fabricated counts — every number (`n_comps`, `n_patterns`, category counts, zip size) is read live from the same variables `build_portal.py` already computes elsewhere in the file; none are hand-typed into `body_home()`.

## 3. Inputs

| Source | What this reads |
|---|---|
| `comps`, `n_comps`, `categories` (already computed, ~line 77–85) | Component count and per-category breakdown for §5.5 |
| `patterns`, `n_patterns` (already computed, ~line 359–375) | Pattern count and titles for §5.6. One addition: capture each pattern's one-line description (the italic sentence already present under the H1 in every `templates/*.md` file — verified present in all five) as a new `"blurb"` field on the pattern dict, so the home page doesn't hand-write copy that duplicates what's already in the source file. |
| `tokens` (`_meta`, `primitive`, `semantic`, `legacy`, `accessibility` — verified top-level keys) | Tier names and one real example path per tier for §5.4 |
| `zip_kb`, download paths (already computed, ~line 384–385, used in `body_downloads()`) | Hero CTA button and closing CTA strip — reuses `downloads/cartrack-ai-design-system.zip` (root-relative, no `../` prefix, since home is at `docs/` root unlike `resources/downloads.html`) |
| Real package root listing (`design-system/`: `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `tokens/tokens.json`, `components/`, `templates/`, `component-library-preview.html`, `vibe-tests/`) | The folder-tree visual in §5.3 — verified against the actual filesystem, not the guide's prose description |

## 4. Proposed page structure

1. **Hero.** Headline stays close to today's ("Build on-system UI with AI"), but the paragraph leads with positioning ("AI-optimized design system" — real Fleet Portal tokens and components, packaged for an AI coding tool) instead of a portal-documentation framing. Primary action is now a real button, not an implied stat strip: **"Download the system"**, same href/attributes as the existing `.dlbtn.primary` CTA on the downloads page (`downloads/cartrack-ai-design-system.zip`, `download` attribute), with a one-line sub-caption ("Unzip, then connect the folder to Claude, Cursor, Copilot, or another AI tool"). Stat strip (component count / tokens source / setup steps) stays underneath, now supporting the CTA rather than replacing it.
2. **How it works — 3 numbered steps.** Reuses `.steps`/`.stepc` (already defined, currently only used on `guides/index.html`). Steps, worded tool-agnostically (see §6 for the specific correction this makes to the user's draft wording):
   1. **Download the package** — grab the zip, unzip anywhere; the top-level folder is the whole thing.
   2. **Point your AI tool at that folder** — connect it as the workspace root in Claude Cowork or Claude Code, or open it as the workspace in Cursor/Copilot/Codex.
   3. **The rules load automatically** — Claude reads `CLAUDE.md`, which imports `AGENTS.md`; other tools read `AGENTS.md` directly via the agents.md standard. From here, just ask for UI in plain language.
3. **What's inside — folder-tree visual.** A new visual component (§7) listing the real root of the package — `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, `tokens/tokens.json`, `components/`, `templates/`, `component-library-preview.html`, `vibe-tests/` — one row per item, file/folder name in mono type, a one-line purpose caption next to each. Per the review decision, the guardrail/trust point (WCAG AA enforced, values pulled from real production code not invented, gaps get flagged not fabricated) is folded in as one closing line under the tree, not a separate section.
4. **Tokens / Foundations.** Explains the token model in enough depth to be useful on its own: the three tiers that actually exist in `tokens.json` (`primitive`, `semantic`, `legacy`) plus the separate `accessibility` block, one real example path per tier, and the one rule that matters most in practice (code uses `primitive.*`, docs cite `semantic.*`). Links to `foundations/index.html` for the full colour/type/spacing pages.
5. **Component library.** The count (`n_comps`) and a breakdown by category (`categories` — Actions, Data display, Feedback, Inputs & forms, Navigation, Surfaces, with their real counts), reusing `.idxgrid`/`.idxcard`-style small cards or a simple pill list. Links to `components/index.html`.
6. **Patterns.** New section (this was the reviewed addition): `n_patterns` full-page compositions, one line per pattern using the newly-captured `blurb` field (§3) — e.g. "Table Page — the workhorse screen of the Fleet Portal: a filterable, paginated collection of records." Links to `patterns/index.html`. Directly supports the "production-optimized UI prototypes" framing, since patterns are what show components composing into whole screens rather than existing in isolation.
7. **Closing CTA strip.** Reviewed addition: a short final block repeating the download button (same href as §5.1) so a visitor who scrolled all the way down doesn't have to scroll back up. One line of copy ("Ready to start?") plus the button — not a repeat of the hero's full copy.
8. **What's here (index grid) + warning callout — kept, demoted.** Same five cards and copy as today, positioned after the closing CTA as secondary wayfinding, not the page's main content.

## 5. What this removes from the current (narrative) build

- "Why this exists," "Proof, not promises," and the condensed `.paths` cards (Designers/Developers/AI agents) — all removed from `body_home()`. Nothing needs to change in `guides/index.html`, which still carries the full versions of the "why" explanation and the three path cards with their try-it prompts.
- The `vibe_proof_line()` helper (added for the Proof block) becomes unused by `body_home()` — recommend deleting it rather than leaving dead code, since nothing else in the file calls it (verified: single call site).
- The `.tagline` CSS rule added for the narrative spec's positioning line can stay; the new hero still uses a short positioning line in the same style.

## 6. Correction to the user's draft wording (step 3)

The user's draft phrasing was "Claude automatically scans CLAUDE.md file." That's accurate for Claude specifically but not for other tools — Cursor, Copilot, and Codex don't read `CLAUDE.md`; they read `AGENTS.md` directly via the agents.md convention (this is already documented correctly on `guides/index.html`'s "What's inside" table). §4 step 3's wording above generalizes this so the home page doesn't imply Claude-only support for a system whose hero explicitly says "Claude, Cursor, Copilot."

## 7. Technical approach

All changes are inside `build_portal.py`:

- **Patterns loader (~line 359–372):** add one line capturing `blurb` — the first non-empty line after the title that isn't a heading (already present as the italic sentence under every `# Title` in `templates/*.md`, verified across all five files). Purely additive; nothing currently reads this field, so no risk to existing pages.
- **`body_home()` (~line 868):** full rewrite per §4. Reuses `.steps`/`.stepc`, `.idxgrid`/`.idxcard`, `.rule`, `.statstrip`, `.tagline`, `.dlbtn` — all already defined in `render_shell()`.
- **New CSS in `render_shell()`:** one new component, the folder-tree visual (§4.3):
  ```
  .ftree{background:var(--card);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:var(--r-lg);overflow:hidden}
  .ftrow{display:grid;grid-template-columns:280px 1fr;gap:16px;padding:11px 22px;border-top:1px solid var(--line);align-items:baseline}
  .ftrow:first-child{border-top:none}
  .ftrow.depth-1{padding-left:46px}
  .ftname{font-family:var(--mono);font-size:13px;color:var(--ink)}
  .ftdesc{font-size:13.5px;color:var(--ink2)}
  ```
  Deliberately reuses the existing card/border/radius language (`var(--card)`, `var(--line)`, `var(--r-lg)`) rather than introducing a new visual vocabulary — consistent with the "no new shape language" principle from `home-page-visual-redesign-spec.md` §3.5.
- **Closing CTA strip:** a `<div class="ctaend">` with the same `.dlbtn` markup as the hero and downloads page; one small CSS rule for centering/spacing, no new colour or button style.
- **Deletion:** remove `vibe_proof_line()` (§5) since its only caller goes away with this rewrite.

## 8. Rollout

1. **Build 1:** Patterns loader `blurb` field (additive, zero risk) + hero rewrite (§4.1) + how-it-works steps (§4.2).
2. **Build 2:** Folder-tree visual (§4.3, new CSS) + tokens/foundations section (§4.4).
3. **Build 3:** Component library section (§4.5) + patterns section (§4.6) + closing CTA (§4.7) + index-grid demotion (§4.8).
4. Regenerate the full portal (one `build_portal.py` run) after each build step and check that no other page changed — `render_shell()` is shared, so any new CSS added there is inherited everywhere even though only home's markup changes.

## 9. Acceptance criteria

- `design-system/` requires zero changes to ship this spec.
- `guides/index.html` is unchanged.
- Every count on the home page (`n_comps`, `n_patterns`, category counts, zip size) is read from the same live variables the rest of the portal already uses — none hand-typed.
- No orphaned code: `vibe_proof_line()` removed once nothing calls it.
- No new WCAG AA violations — new sections reuse already-audited colour/type pairs; the one new CSS component (`.ftree`) uses existing card/border/text colour variables only.
- Regenerating the portal after this change is still exactly one `build_portal.py` run, and every other generated page is byte-identical apart from the shared `render_shell()` CSS block picking up the new `.ftree`/`.ctaend` rules.
